Session.title: Session
Session.description: Represents a session where students are grouped.

hostId.description: Reference to User who is hosting the session. (Relationship: User 1:N Session)

status.description: Status of the session (e.g., lobby, grouped).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the session was created.

groups.description: Array of groups within the session. Each group contains an array of participants.

Participant.title: Participant
Participant.description: Represents a participant in a session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the participant (same as User ID).

sessionId.description: Reference to Session that the participant is in. (Relationship: Session 1:N Participant)

joinedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the participant joined the session.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application.

definition.description: Stores session data, including the host ID, status, creation timestamp, and group assignments. The 'hostId' field is denormalized for authorization independence.

params.name: appId
params.description: The unique identifier for the application.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the session.

definition.description: Stores participant data within a session, including the participant's name, avatar, and join timestamp.

params.name: appId
params.description: The unique identifier for the application.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the session.

params.name: participantId
params.description: The unique identifier for the participant (same as User ID).

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the GroupSync application's features, focusing on session management, participant tracking, and group assignments. The structure is optimized for real-time updates and efficient data retrieval while adhering to the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-Based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules are not Filters). 

**Authorization Independence:**
*   The `sessions` collection stores session metadata, including `hostId`. The `hostId` is denormalized from the `User` entity to the `Session` entity, enabling session creation without needing to read the user document.
*   The `participants` subcollection under each session contains participant details. This eliminates the need to perform `get()` calls to determine the host or participant details during session or participant creation, facilitating atomic operations.

**Structural Segregation & QAPs:**
*   The app uses a dedicated collection `artifacts/{appId}/public/data/sessions/{sessionId}` for sessions and `artifacts/{appId}/public/data/sessions/{sessionId}/participants/{participantId}` for participants. This structural segregation enables straightforward security rules that can efficiently secure list operations.
*   Path-based ownership is leveraged for securing user-related data. The sessions are not directly nested under users but contain the `hostId` attribute for easy querying. This approach makes the data easily queryable and scalable.

**Access Modeling:**
*   Path-based ownership is used for managing sessions and participants. The `/sessions/{sessionId}` path contains session data, and the `/sessions/{sessionId}/participants/{participantId}` path contains participant data within a session.
*   No membership maps are used as this app has a simple ownership and participation structure.

**Data Clarity and Predictability:**
*   Explicit state modeling is used with the `status` field in the `Session` entity, clearly indicating the session's state (e.g., 'lobby', 'grouped').
*   The schema for each entity (`Session`, `Participant`, `User`) is clearly defined, promoting consistency and predictability.
